children.finalMessage: {"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"Correctness review found live-execution edge cases around non-persistent kill-switch state, dispatch races, idempotency races, revoked CEX connection reactivation, and invalid notional handling.","findings":[{"file":"src/server/execution/riskEngine.ts","line_start":31,"line_end":40,"severity":"high","title":"Global kill switch is process-local and can reset or diverge","description":"GLOBAL_KILL is held only in module memory. In a Workers/serverless runtime, new isolates, restarts, or parallel instances can see the default false value even after an admin enables the global kill switch, allowing execution to continue unexpectedly.","recommendation":"Persist the global kill state in durable storage such as D1/KV/Durable Object and read it inside decideExecution before approving any order."},{"file":"src/server/execution/router.ts","line_start":58,"line_end":62,"severity":"high","title":"Dispatch can fan out the wrong intent under concurrent inserts","description":"After inserting a trade intent, the code selects the latest global tradeIntents row by descending id. If two admins or automated sources insert concurrently, this request can load and dispatch another request's intent.","recommendation":"Use an insert-returning pattern if supported, or generate a unique external/client id and select by that id plus createdBy/source instead of selecting the global latest row."},{"file":"src/server/execution/dispatch.ts","line_start":70,"line_end":132,"severity":"critical","title":"Idempotency check is race-prone and lacks a database uniqueness guard","description":"The duplicate check and insert are separate operations, while schema/migrations define idempotencyKey as non-unique. Concurrent redispatches or separate Worker instances can both pass the check and insert/submit duplicate jobs for the same user and intent, causing duplicated live orders.","recommendation":"Add a unique constraint on the execution idempotency key, preferably scoped to provider/user/intent as needed, and use insert-on-conflict/transactional semantics so only one job can be created."},{"file":"src/server/cex/store.ts","line_start":134,"line_end":167,"severity":"high","title":"Validation can reactivate revoked CEX credentials","description":"validateCexConnection selects the latest row for user and exchange without filtering status. After a user revokes a connection, calling the validate endpoint for that exchange can decrypt the revoked row and set its status back to active.","recommendation":"Validate only pending or active rows according to the intended flow, reject revoked rows explicitly, and require a new connect operation to create fresh pending credentials after revocation."},{"file":"src/server/execution/riskEngine.ts","line_start":90,"line_end":139,"severity":"high","title":"Invalid notional strings can propagate as NaN into live order creation","description":"requestedNotionalUsd is accepted as a string and parsed without Number.isFinite validation. Values like 'abc' become NaN, bypass the negative check, return an approved decision, and can later produce quantity/notional values of 'NaN' because NaN comparisons do not fail the zero-quantity guard.","recommendation":"Validate requestedNotionalUsd as a positive finite numeric value at the router boundary and repeat finite checks in riskEngine and dispatch before approving or submitting an order."}],"next_steps":["Persist global execution safety state before relying on the kill switch.","Add database-level idempotency constraints and update dispatch to handle conflicts atomically.","Tighten CEX connection state transitions so revoked credentials cannot be reactivated.","Add finite positive numeric validation for all money and price inputs before risk approval or order sizing."]}

{"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"Security review found multiple trust-boundary issues around live trading controls, public maintenance endpoints, tenant isolation, email verification, and secret encryption. The most serious issue is that unauthenticated HTTP endpoints can trigger scraper execution that can fan out live CEX orders.","findings":[{"file":"src/server/worker.ts","line_start":28,"line_end":89,"severity":"critical","title":"Unauthenticated maintenance endpoints can trigger live trading side effects","description":"Public POST endpoints for /api/scraper/run, /api/scraper/backfill, and /api/outcome/validate run database-mutating jobs without authentication. runCoinlegsScraper creates trade intents and dispatches execution jobs, so any internet caller could trigger expensive jobs and potentially live orders for connected CEX accounts.","recommendation":"Require admin authentication for all manual job endpoints, reject requests when the admin secret is unset, add rate limiting, and separate read-only validation from execution/dispatch side effects."},{"file":"src/server/worker.ts","line_start":105,"line_end":112,"severity":"high","title":"Admin endpoint becomes public when ADMIN_API_KEY is unset","description":"The fee crystallization endpoint only rejects when expected is truthy and mismatched. If ADMIN_API_KEY is missing or empty, any caller can run fee crystallization.","recommendation":"Fail closed when ADMIN_API_KEY is not configured, compare secrets with a constant-time comparison, and return 503/500 for misconfiguration before doing any work."},{"file":"src/server/routers.ts","line_start":245,"line_end":257,"severity":"high","title":"Any authenticated user can control and inspect global Binance account state","description":"The Binance router uses protectedProcedure rather than adminProcedure for global settings, kill switch changes, balance reads, and execution history. These functions operate on global BINANCE_API_KEY credentials and global trade_executions rather than tenant-scoped user data.","recommendation":"Restrict global Binance endpoints to adminProcedure or remove them in favor of per-user CEX connections. Scope execution reads and settings by user where applicable."},{"file":"src/server/routers.ts","line_start":57,"line_end":66,"severity":"high","title":"Registration auto-verifies email ownership","description":"The register mutation creates a verification token and immediately calls verifyEmailToken before issuing the session cookie. This marks arbitrary registered emails as verified without proving mailbox ownership.","recommendation":"Do not call verifyEmailToken during registration. Send the token out-of-band, keep emailVerified=false until the user returns the token, and consider limiting sensitive actions until verification is complete."},{"file":"src/server/db.ts","line_start":34,"line_end":65,"severity":"medium","title":"Exchange credentials use unauthenticated AES-CBC with weak key separation","description":"API keys and signer private keys are encrypted with AES-CBC, which does not authenticate ciphertext, and ENCRYPTION_KEY falls back to JWT_SECRET with padding/truncation. This risks ciphertext tampering and couples session signing material with data encryption.","recommendation":"Use AES-GCM or another AEAD mode with a required, dedicated ENCRYPTION_KEY in production. Derive keys with HKDF, include key/version metadata, and reject startup if the encryption secret is missing or too weak."},{"file":"src/server/aster/store.ts","line_start":104,"line_end":135,"severity":"medium","title":"Aster approval state trusts client-reported booleans","description":"recordAsterApprovals marks an agent active solely from authenticated user-supplied agentApproved and builderApproved flags. It does not verify approvals against Aster/on-chain state before activating the live account.","recommendation":"Treat the client flags as UI hints only. Verify agent and builder permissions server-side against the provider before setting status=active or enabling live execution."}],"next_steps":["Lock down all manual job and fee endpoints before deployment.","Convert global Binance routes to admin-only or tenant-scoped routes.","Fix registration so email verification requires out-of-band proof.","Migrate encrypted credentials to AEAD with a dedicated required secret.","Add security tests for unauthenticated job endpoints, admin-only controls, tenant-scoped execution reads, and email verification."]}

{"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"The change is not ready to ship. The strongest blockers are around money-firing paths: unauthenticated operational endpoints can trigger live dispatch work, idempotency is only best-effort without a database constraint, kill switches are not durable or enforced at the final exchange-submit boundary, and revoked exchange credentials can be reactivated.","findings":[{"file":"src/server/worker.ts","line_start":28,"line_end":84,"severity":"critical","title":"Unauthenticated HTTP endpoints can trigger scraper and live dispatch work","description":"The manual scraper and outcome validation routes are plain Hono POST endpoints with no session, role, or API-key check. /api/scraper/run calls runCoinlegsScraper(), and that path can create trade intents and fan them out to active CEX connections. A public caller can repeatedly trigger operational work against live trading infrastructure.","recommendation":"Put these endpoints behind the same admin authorization model as dispatchIntent, or require a mandatory Worker secret/API key. Fail closed when the secret is absent, and add rate limiting for operational triggers."},{"file":"src/server/cex/store.ts","line_start":134,"line_end":170,"severity":"high","title":"Revoked CEX connections can be revalidated back to active","description":"validateCexConnection selects the newest row for a user/exchange without filtering out revoked connections, then unconditionally updates that row to status active after validation. After a user revokes an exchange, calling the validate mutation for that exchange can resurrect the revoked credential set.","recommendation":"Validate by connection id owned by the caller and require status pending or active, never revoked. Add a WHERE status IN (...) guard to the update and return an error when the latest connection is revoked."},{"file":"src/drizzle/schema.ts","line_start":309,"line_end":309,"severity":"critical","title":"Execution idempotency is not enforced by the database","description":"execution_jobs.idempotencyKey is not unique, while dispatch uses a select-then-insert check before submitting real orders. Concurrent cron/manual redispatches or multiple Worker isolates can both miss the existing row and insert duplicate jobs, causing duplicate live orders for the same user and signal.","recommendation":"Add a composite unique index on userId + idempotencyKey, change job creation to insert-on-conflict/do-nothing, and only submit the order for the row this invocation successfully created."},{"file":"src/server/execution/riskEngine.ts","line_start":31,"line_end":40,"severity":"high","title":"Global kill switch is process-local and will not reliably halt execution","description":"The global kill switch is a module-level boolean. In Cloudflare Workers this state is per isolate and resets on cold starts/deploys, so setting it through one request does not guarantee scheduled jobs or another isolate will observe it. The code claims this halts all execution, but it is not a durable control plane.","recommendation":"Persist the global kill switch in D1/KV/Durable Object and read it inside decideExecution and immediately before exchange submission. Treat read failures as kill-switch active."},{"file":"src/server/cex/adapter.ts","line_start":21,"line_end":46,"severity":"high","title":"Per-user kill switches are not checked at final order submission","description":"Risk checks happen before the job is queued, but the adapter only reloads the connection and checks status === active before placing the exchange order. If a user toggles killSwitchActive or disables copytrade while a job is queued or retrying, the order can still be submitted.","recommendation":"Reload the connection and live account immediately before every placeOrder call and abort unless status is active, copytrade is enabled, and all relevant kill switches are off. Mark the job canceled/skipped rather than submitting."},{"file":"src/server/db.ts","line_start":41,"line_end":65,"severity":"high","title":"Exchange keys and signer private keys use unauthenticated AES-CBC encryption","description":"encryptKey/decryptKey use AES-CBC without an authentication tag for stored API keys and signer private keys. Ciphertext tampering is not detected, and CBC is the wrong primitive for secret custody in a trading system.","recommendation":"Migrate encrypted secrets to an AEAD scheme such as AES-GCM with random nonce and auth tag, include a key/version prefix, and reject legacy ciphertext after a controlled rotation."}],"next_steps":["Lock down public operational endpoints before any live deployment.","Add database-backed idempotency and durable kill-switch state.","Move final exchange submission behind a last-moment guardrail check.","Migrate secret encryption to authenticated encryption and add regression tests for revoke, duplicate dispatch, and kill-switch races."]}

{"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"Performance review found several scaling bottlenecks: missing indexes on hot tables, N+1 database patterns in scraper/demo/fee/execution paths, repeated full-table analytics in request handlers, and one frontend timer leak.","findings":[{"file":"src/drizzle/schema.ts","line_start":130,"line_end":154,"severity":"high","title":"Hot Coinlegs queries lack supporting indexes","description":"coinlegs_signals is queried by signal, qualityTier, period, exchg, qualityScore, signalDate, marketName, scrapedAt, and outcomeValidated, but the schema only declares a unique signalId. As signal history grows, dashboard pagination, performance stats, scraper dedup/confluence checks, and outcome validation will degrade into table scans and temp sorts.","recommendation":"Add composite indexes for the actual access patterns, for example signal/qualityTier/period/exchg with qualityScore and signalDate ordering, marketName/period/scrapedAt for confluence, outcomeValidated/signal/scrapedAt for validation, plus matching indexes on demoTrades, portfolioSnapshots, executionJobs, navSnapshots, feePeriods, liveAccounts, and cexConnections."},{"file":"src/server/coinlegs-scraper.ts","line_start":206,"line_end":267,"severity":"high","title":"Scraper does per-signal database work and O(n²) batch scans","description":"For every fetched signal, the scraper performs a dedup query, scans fetched.signals with filter() to compute same-group confluence, optionally performs another recent-signal query, then inserts one row. For n fetched signals this is O(n²) CPU plus roughly 2n sequential database round trips before inserts.","recommendation":"Precompute current-batch confluence in a Map keyed by marketName|period, load recent DB confluence rows for all keys in one query, wrap inserts in a transaction, and use batch insert/on-conflict behavior instead of per-row duplicate probes."},{"file":"src/server/execution/dispatch.ts","line_start":53,"line_end":85,"severity":"high","title":"Trade intent fan-out is sequential across every active CEX connection","description":"createExecutionJobsForIntent loads all active connections and processes them one at a time. Each connection calls decideExecution, checks idempotency, decrypts credentials, calls the exchange balance API, then re-queries liveAccounts. Since the scraper can call this once per Tier A signal, latency grows as O(signals * activeConnections) with blocking network I/O in the request path.","recommendation":"Move fan-out into a bounded-concurrency background queue, bulk-load live accounts/nav exposure for all candidate users, cache per-connection balance within the dispatch cycle, and reserve sequential execution only for per-connection order submission."},{"file":"src/server/execution/riskEngine.ts","line_start":46,"line_end":113,"severity":"medium","title":"Risk checks perform multiple queries per user during dispatch","description":"decideExecution executes separate queries for liveAccounts, today's nav snapshots, open execution jobs, and latest nav for each user. When called inside the active-connection loop, this becomes a classic N+1 query pattern and duplicates data that could be fetched once for the dispatch batch.","recommendation":"Provide a batch risk-evaluation path that preloads accounts, latest/today nav snapshots, and open job exposure for all userIds, then computes decisions in memory from indexed grouped results."},{"file":"src/server/db.ts","line_start":355,"line_end":419,"severity":"medium","title":"Demo sync scales as accounts times signals with per-signal writes","description":"syncSignalsToDemoAccounts loops through every active demo account, fetches new signals for each account, and inserts demoTrades one row at a time. If pyramiding is enabled, it also runs a count query per signal. This will become expensive as both demo accounts and historical signals grow.","recommendation":"Batch accounts by strategy tier and lastSyncedSignalId, prefetch pyramid counts grouped by account/pair/window, bulk insert demoTrades and snapshots in a transaction, and add indexes on demoTrades(demoAccountId,pair,openedAt) and coinlegsSignals(id,signal,qualityTier,signalDate)."},{"file":"src/server/db.ts","line_start":578,"line_end":740,"severity":"medium","title":"Performance endpoint materializes and reprocesses all signals per request","description":"getPerformance loads all signal rows needed for analytics into memory, then repeatedly filters, groups, and sorts arrays to compute medians and breakdowns. This is CPU- and memory-heavy on every request and gets worse linearly with signal history, with extra O(k log k) sorts for medians.","recommendation":"Move these aggregations to SQL where practical, maintain rollup tables when new scraper data lands, or cache the computed performance payload with invalidation after scraper runs."},{"file":"src/components/TypewriterHeading.tsx","line_start":28,"line_end":36,"severity":"low","title":"Typewriter interval can survive unmount","description":"The cleanup returned from inside the setTimeout callback is ignored by React. If the component unmounts after the interval has started, clearTimeout(timer) will not clear the active interval, so it can keep firing setState on an unmounted component.","recommendation":"Store the interval and onDone timeout ids in refs or outer-scope variables and clear them from the effect cleanup alongside the initial delay timer."}],"next_steps":["Add the missing database indexes before increasing scraper cadence or user count.","Refactor scraper and dispatch paths to batch database reads and use bounded concurrency for external calls.","Cache or materialize performance analytics instead of recomputing them from all signal rows per request.","Fix the TypewriterHeading timer cleanup to prevent interval leaks."]}
